I agree with the statement "Osteoporosis is not associated with Paget's disease." One additional point to support this would be that Paget's disease often presents with high bone mass and thickened trabeculae, contrasting sharply with the low bone mineral density characteristic of osteoporosis where there is reduced bone formation and accelerated bone loss. I agree with the statement "Osteoporosis is not associated with Paget's disease." One additional point to support this would be that Paget's disease often presents with high bone mass and thickened trabeculae, contrasting sharply with the low bone mineral density characteristic of osteoporosis where there is reduced bone formation and accelerated bone loss. The different pathophysiological mechanisms underlying both conditions also reinforce their distinct associations within skeletal health disorders.
